$comment: CURRENT-2424 pilot eval set (Categories + Collections subset of the eventual fixed 25-prompt set). Every `expected` was verified against api-reference/storefront-v2026-04.yaml. Path templates use the same {placeholder} names as the spec; the grader treats any {placeholder} or concrete value in a placeholder segment as equivalent. auth: 'none' = spec `security: []` (public storefront surface), 'bearer' = spec `bearer_auth` (company surface).

prompts.prompt: I'm building an unauthenticated storefront browse page. What API call lists the categories that are live on a company's public storefront? I just need the public catalog for the current shop, no login.
prompts.notes: Public Categories catalog. Spec line 3492-3504: storefrontCategoriesIndex, security: []. Company resolved from subdomain, so no auth.

prompts.prompt: I have a collection's URL slug ("wellness-essentials") and need to render that single collection on a public, no-login storefront page. What's the request to fetch it by slug?
prompts.notes: Public Collection by slug. Spec line 6609-6620: storefrontCollectionShow, security: []. Resolves by slug path param.

prompts.prompt: As a company admin with an API bearer token, how do I create a brand-new category titled "Summer Sale"?
prompts.notes: Create a Category. Spec line 3711-3731: companyCategoriesCreate, bearer_auth. Body wrapped in `category`; required field is `title` (CategoryCreateRequest line 590-598).

prompts.prompt: Using our company admin token, I need to rename an existing collection (id 5102) to "Holiday Picks". What's the exact request?
prompts.notes: Update a Collection. Spec line 6800-6822: companyCollectionsUpdate uses PATCH, bearer_auth. Body wrapped in `collection`; rename sets `title` (CollectionWrite line 2971).

prompts.prompt: On the public (no-auth) storefront, fetch the first 50 top-level categories that are available in the United Kingdom, returned in French, sorted alphabetically by title (A to Z). What's the full request with query parameters?
prompts.notes: Public Categories catalog with filters. filter[country]=GB (line 118-119), filter[language]=fr (line 130-131), filter[parent_id]=root for top-level (line 3518-3521), sort=title (line 166-175), page[limit]=50 (line 107-108). All names verified verbatim.

prompts.prompt: How do I permanently delete the category with id 4821 as a company admin (I have a bearer token)?
prompts.notes: Destroy a Category. Spec line 3888-3902: companyCategoriesDestroy, DELETE, bearer_auth, returns 204.

prompts.prompt: I want the category with id 4821 to automatically go live at 2026-08-01T08:00:00Z. As a company admin, what single call schedules it to publish at that time?
prompts.notes: Scheduling is done via update (there is no separate publish endpoint). Spec line 3847-3871: companyCategoriesUpdate PATCH, bearer. Set status=scheduled + publish_at (CategoryWrite line 533-538). Body wrapped in `category`.

prompts.prompt: We're only selling a collection (id 5102) in Great Britain and the United States. As a company admin, how do I restrict that collection's country availability to just GB and US?
prompts.notes: Country availability is a write field, not a query param, on the company surface. Spec CollectionWrite line 2999-3005: country_isos array restricts availability. Set via companyCollectionsUpdate PATCH (line 6800). Body wrapped in `collection`.

prompts.prompt: On the public storefront, how do I fetch only the child categories nested directly under the parent category whose id is 4821?
prompts.notes: Hierarchy / children-of filter. Spec line 3518-3524: filter[parent_id] takes a category id (or 'root' for top-level). Only exists on the public categories index, not the company index. Value would be 4821.

prompts.prompt: As a company admin, how do I pull the aggregate share statistics for the category with id 4821?
prompts.notes: Share statistics for a Category. Spec line 3913-3922: companyCategoriesShares, GET, bearer_auth, read-only aggregate.
